title: Coniogramme merrillii
intro: Coniogramme merrillii, also known as Merrill's maidenhair fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and creeping rhizome. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Coniogramme merrillii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Coniogramme merrillii is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Coniogramme merrillii is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Coniogramme, which is a small group of ferns native to tropical Asia. Coniogramme merrillii is a type of evergreen fern, known for its small size and delicate fronds. It is typically found growing in shaded areas, often in association with other tropical plants. The classification of Coniogramme merrillii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Pteridaceae family.
